---
title: "My Quarto Analysis"
format: html
---

## **Text and images**

You can add text here just like a word processor.

![](images/Composition%20Workflow-1.png){width="640"}

## Equations

The equation is $E = mc^2$. Mix of text and Math as, The mean of the numbers is $\bar{x} = `r mean(c(1,2,3))`$.

### Numbered equations

$$
E = mc^2
$$ {#eq-energy}

### Reference it:

See Equation @eq-energy.

### Aligned equations

Equations can align as : $$
\begin{aligned}
y &= mx + b \\
z &= ax^2 + bx + c
\end{aligned}
$$

## Tables

### A. Simple Markdown Tables

| Name | Age | Score |
|------|-----|-------|
| John | 25  | 90    |
| Mary | 30  | 95    |

: Note: Simple Markdown tables cannot be cross-referenced in Quarto.

### B. Tables from R (MOST COMMON)

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
# Load libraries and create data
library(knitr)
students <- data.frame(
  Name = c("John", "Mary"),
  Age = c(25, 30),
  Score = c(90, 95)
)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-students
#| tbl-cap: "Student Scores"
   kable(students)
```

Reference as:

See Table @tbl-students.

## Code Chunks

Code that appears in your rendered doc

```{r my-chunk, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
```

`{# This code will appear in the final rendered document} print("Hello Quarto!")`

Codes that are excluded from the final document

```{r fig-starwars, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.cap="Relationship between height and mass of Star Wars characters"}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

starwars %>%
  filter(mass < 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mass, y = height, color = gender)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Height vs Mass of Star Wars Characters",
    x = "Mass",
    y = "Height",
    color = "Gender"
  )
```

## Code included in Text

The average height of star-wars characters is `r round(mean(starwars$height,na.rm=TRUE)/100,2)` in meters.

## Cross Referencing

Here is a reference to @fig-starwars

## How about a call out?

::: callout-warning
## remember to use sunscreen
:::

## Add a bibliography

Here is some text with citations about R programming .

## Bibliography

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:
